mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or remains at a very low level, discount rates ease and equity valuation multiples expand, so the S&P 500 should post positive 20-day forward returns. The signal fails if rate cuts are already fully priced or if easing coincides with acute recession stress.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of FEDFUNDS-decline observations, or if the signal's rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above a short-term threshold signals weakening household income and demand, which should depress near-term equity returns as analysts cut earnings expectations. The effect is negative over the next month and should strengthen when unemployment is rising rather than flat.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3pp over the prior 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 45% of cases, or if the sign flips positive after controlling for the level of FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized improvement in PAYEMS and RSAFS indicates expanding payroll income and retail demand, which should lift cyclical earnings expectations and push the S&P 500 higher over the following month. The mechanism is strongest when both series are rising together rather than individually.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint-positive PAYEMS/RSAFS state does not outperform single-series states by at least 5 percentage points in hit rate, or if 20-day forward returns are not positive on average in triggered joint-improvement windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in INDPRO combined with weak HOUST implies industrial capacity is tightening faster than housing supply, a mix consistent with growth scarcity and margin pressure for equities. Under this stress pattern, S&P 500 forward returns should be negative within 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO is up while HOUST falls by at least 5% over the prior 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if the specified joint condition does not precede negative 5-day SPX returns in at least 60% of cases, or if the median return is not below zero.

mechanism_narrative: A slowdown in GDPC1 growth indicates weaker aggregate demand and lower expected corporate revenue growth, which should reduce equity prices over the next quarter. The signal is negative because GDP deceleration transmits to earnings revisions with a lag longer than one month.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth turns negative versus the prior quarter
failure_condition: Reject if negative GDPC1 growth quarters are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the effect disappears after two consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS indicates inflation pressure followed by active policy tightening, which should compress equity multiples and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The mechanism fails when inflation accelerates but the Fed does not respond.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates while FEDFUNDS is already above 4.5%
failure_condition: Reject if the joint inflation-plus-high-rate regime does not yield negative 20-day SPX returns in at least 55% of observations, or if the average return is nonnegative.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent rise in PAYEMS after a labor-market trough signals improving hiring and income, which should support equity returns over the next 60 trading days as spending and margins recover. The effect is positive when payroll growth is broad and sustained, not a one-month bounce.
failure_condition: Reject if 3-month payroll improvement episodes do not lead to positive 60-day SPX returns in at least 60% of cases, or if the signal weakens to zero after the first 20 days.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and HOUST both improve, consumer spending and residential activity confirm a broad domestic demand upswing that should lift S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal fails if retail strength is isolated while housing remains weak, because the joint demand impulse is missing.
failure_condition: Reject if joint RSAFS-and-HOUST improvement does not outperform non-joint states by at least 5 percentage points in 20-day hit rate, or if the average forward return is not positive.
